perturbation.original_text: Applicable Definitions. a. Confidential Information. As used herein, the term “Confidential Information” includes (1) information that contains sensitive financial, commercial, or other proprietar y business information concerning or relating to the TJPA or the Project; (2) information that contains sensitive financial, commercial, or other proprietary business information received from third parties under Non -Disclosure and/or Confidentiality Agree ments; or (3) information that the TJPA has determined, in its sole discretion, to constitute confidential, privileged, sensitive, or proprietary information concerning the Project’s security, operations, or facilities.
perturbation.changed_text: Applicable Definitions. a. Confidential Information. As used herein, the term “Confidential Information” includes (1) information that contains sensitive financial, commercial, or other proprietar y business information concerning or relating to the TJPA or the Project; (2) information that contains sensitive financial, commercial, or other proprietary business information received from third parties under Non -Disclosure and/or Confidentiality Agree ments; or (3) information that the TJPA has determined, in its sole discretion, to constitute non-confidential, non-privileged, non-sensitive, or non-proprietary information concerning the Project’s security, operations, or facilities.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the characteristics of information that TJPA determines, in its sole discretion, this clause contradicts the overall intent of the agreement to protect sensitive information. Section 2 which states that "Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be held and treated in the strictest confidence in a manner designed to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to third parties" is now contradicted, because the definition of Confidential Information now includes non-confidential information. This creates ambiguity about which information is actually protected under the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure Of Confidential Information, SSI, Or PCII. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be held and treated in the strictest confidence in a manner designed to prevent the unauthorized disclosure to third parties. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII may appear in records in written or electronic form, including but not limited to, drawings, photographs, schematics, plans, memorandums, reports, emails, videos, tape recordings, and Powerpoint presentations. Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII shall not be disclosed to third parties without the express written permission of the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee; and if such permission is granted, then said information will be provided only upon the execution of a Non- Disclosure Agreement by that third party. If at any time Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII is disclosed in violation of this Agreement, including but not limited to any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation that the Designated Individual has knowledge of (whether or not he/she is personally involved), the Designated Individual shall immediately give the TJPA written notice of that fact and a detailed account of the circumstances regarding such disclosure. The Designated Individual’s anonymity shall be kept to the extent possible when reporting security violations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' to include 'non-confidential, non-privileged, non-sensitive, or non-proprietary information'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which states that 'Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be held and treated in the strictest confidence'. Including non-confidential information within the definition of Confidential Information creates a clear contradiction and ambiguity about which information is actually protected, making the agreement's intent unclear and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Sensitive Security Information o r SSI. As used herein, the terms “Sensitive Security Information” or “SSI” includes information about security, operations, facilities or other assets or capital projects whose disclosure would be detrimental to the security of TJPA’s employees or patron s, or would invade an individual’s privacy, or reveal trade secrets or privileged or confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: Sensitive Security Information o r SSI. As used herein, the terms “Sensitive Security Information” or “SSI” includes information about security, operations, facilities or other assets or capital projects whose disclosure would be beneficial to the security of TJPA’s employees or patron s, and would protect an individual’s privacy, and not reveal trade secrets or privileged or confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of SSI to include information whose disclosure would be 'beneficial' and 'protect' individual's privacy, contradicts Section 7, which outlines remedies for unauthorized disclosure of SSI. The modified definition suggests that disclosure of SSI would be positive, conflicting with the intent of the NDA to prevent such disclosures. The original intent was to avoid the disclosure to an unauthorized person(s). Now, we have disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violations And Remedies. Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Contractor or any Designated Individual knows or has a reasonable belief that documents subject to the Specification have been or may be disclosed to an unauthorized person(s), the Contractor and/or Designated Individual shall imme diately notify the TJPA. The TJPA shall make the final determination as to whether a violation of the Specification occurred and the course of action to address the violation. Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall, at the TJPA’s election, be subject to any of the following: (1) written reprimand of the Contractor and/or Designated Individual; (2) termination of the Contractor or Designated Individual’s access to Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII; (3) directive to the Contractor to immediately suspended the Designated Individual from performing any further work relating to the Project; (4) if a contract has been awarded, treatment of the unautho rized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII as a material breach of the contract, warranting the termination of the contract for cause; (5) commencement of legal proceedings for any other legal rights and remedies, including injunctive relie f and monetary damages arising from the breach of contract; (6) reporting of any violation related to SSI or PCII to the Department of Homeland Security, the Transportation Security Administration, or the Department of Transportation (these agencies may also independently assess penalties and/or take corrective action as provided under Federal law, including 49 C.F.R. §§ 15.17 and 1520.17); (7) disqualification of a Contractor from bidding on any work for the Project; and/or (8) written directive to retriev e all Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII disclosed to unauthorized persons at Contractor’s expense and to cease future unauthorized disclosure. If legal proceedings to enforce any remedy set forth under this paragraph are commenced by the TJPA, the prevailing party in such legal action shall be entitled to reasonable attorney’s fees and costs . Furthermore, Contractor agrees to indemnify the TJPA for any expenses or damages (including attorney’s fees or costs) incurred by the TJPA arising out of or relating to the Contractor and/or Designated Individual’s unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Sensitive Security Information' (SSI) to include information whose disclosure would be 'beneficial' and 'protect' individual's privacy. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines remedies and penalties for 'Unauthorized Disclosure' of SSI. The original text defined SSI as information whose disclosure would be detrimental, aligning with the 'Unauthorized Disclosure' clause. The changed text now suggests disclosure of SSI is positive, which conflicts with the intent of preventing such disclosures outlined in the contradicted text, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Protected Critical Infrastructure Information or PCII. As used herein, the terms “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information” or “PCII” includes information that is not customarily in the public domain and relates to the security of critical infrastructure or protected systems and includes information regarding systems, facilities, or operational security, or that is proprietary, business sensitive, or which might be used to identify a person who submits such information to a federal agency, such as the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Critical Infrastructure Information or PCII. As used herein, the terms “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information” or “PCII” includes information that is customarily in the public domain and does not relate to the security of critical infrastructure or protected systems and excludes information regarding systems, facilities, or operational security, or that is not proprietary, not business sensitive, or which might be used to identify a person who submits such information to a federal agency, such as the Department of Homeland Security.
perturbation.explanation: By defining PCII as information customarily in the public domain and not related to security and no sensitive information, contradicts Section 3, which restricts the use and handling of PCII. The modified definition would imply that PCII does not require special protection, conflicting with the section's requirements. The original intent of PCII was related to the security of critical infastructure and not for public domain, the pertubation now implies a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use and Handling of Information. Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII so marked shall be used solely for the purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the wor k at the Project and/or performing work at the Project, and shall not be used for any other purpose without the express written consent by the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee. Confidential Information, SSI, and PCII shall be handled and safe guarded by the Designated Individual in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of infor mation. The Designated Individual shall not alter or remove the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” which may appear on documents that the Designated Indiv idual reviews. Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII may be used for the sole purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the work at the Project and/or performing work at the Project. If the Designated Individual must use Confidential In formation, SSI, or PCII to create derivative documents for the purpose of preparing and submitting a bid for a portion of the work and/or performing work at the Project, the appropriate protective marking shall be carried forward to the derivative document s. All derivative documents shall be protected in the same manner as the original documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines PCII as information in the public domain and not related to security, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement for special handling and safeguarding of PCII. The contradicted text states that PCII 'shall be handled and safe guarded' and 'shall not be used for any other purpose without the express written consent,' which is in direct conflict with the new definition of PCII being in the public domain and not related to security. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the changed definition implies that PCII does not need protection, while the contradicted section mandates strict protection measures.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII shall not be disclosed to third parties without the express written permission of the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee; and if such permission is granted, then said information will be provided only upon the execution of a Non- Disclosure Agreement by that third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII shall be disclosed to third parties without the express written permission of the Executive Director of the TJPA or her designee; and if such permission is granted, then said information will be provided without the execution of a Non- Disclosure Agreement by that third party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'not' in "shall not be disclosed" and removing the requirement for a Non-Disclosure Agreement, I have directly contradicted the core principle of the entire agreement, which is to protect sensitive information. This alteration conflicts with Section 8, which establishes the duration and survival of confidentiality obligations. This directly violates the essence of the agreement, putting the security of the Project at risk.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duration And Survival Of Confidentiality Obligations. The obligations under this Agreement shall be perpetual (unless otherwise provided for herein) or until such time as the information is no longer considered Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII and a written notice to that fact is provided to the Designa ted Individual and/or the Contractor by the Executive Director of the TJPA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text mandates that Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII shall *not* be disclosed without written permission and a Non-Disclosure Agreement. The changed text removes the 'not' making disclosure permissible without permission and eliminates the NDA requirement. This directly clashes with the contradicted text which establishes perpetual confidentiality obligations. Thus a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Designated Individual shall not alter or remove the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” which may appear on documents that the Designated Indiv idual reviews.
perturbation.changed_text: The Designated Individual shall alter or remove the protective markings of “Confidential Information,” “Sensitive Security Information,” or “Protected Critical Infrastructure Information,” which may appear on documents that the Designated Indiv idual reviews.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall not alter or remove" to "shall alter or remove" directly contradicts the intended protection of sensitive information. This conflicts with Section 6, which specifies retention limitations and the return of information. Removing protective markings would make it impossible to track and control the dissemination of sensitive data as the individual is being encouraged to remove the protective markings from these sensitive documents. This also undermines the requirement to destroy or return documents containing Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Retention Limitations; Return Of Information. Upon the earlier occurrence of: (1) the TJPA’s written request; (2) ten (10) business days after Final Completion of the Project (or for non -successful Bidders, within ten (10) business days after the award of the Trade Work subcontract); (3) upon th e conclusion of the Designated Individual’s duties, association, or support to the Contractor in connection with the Project; or (4) upon the determination that the Designated Individual’s official duties do not require further access to Confidential Infor mation, SSI, or PCII, any and all documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII in the possession of the Designated Individual shall be promptly destroyed , or, if requested by the TJPA, returned to the TJPA . Upon returning or destroying the a forementioned documents, the Designated Individual shall sign the Affidavit of Return or Destruction in the form set forth in Section 01 35 70/AT2. The Designated Individual understands and acknowledges that for documents returned to the TJPA, it is within the TJPA’s sole discretion to determine whether to destroy or maintain any returned documents. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if required by law, the Designated Individual may retain documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII only if: (1) the TJPA is notified in writing of such retention; and (2) the Designated Individual continues to abide by the requirements of this Agreement with respect to protection of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Designated Individual 'shall not alter or remove' protective markings. The changed text mandates that the Designated Individual 'shall alter or remove' those markings. This directly contradicts Section 6, which requires the Designated Individual to destroy or return documents containing Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII, and sign an affidavit confirming this. Removing the markings makes it impossible to identify and properly handle sensitive documents, creating a direct and irreconcilable conflict.

perturbation.original_text: If a subpoena, discovery request, Court order, Freedom Of Information Act Request, or any other request or demand authorized by law seeking disclosure of the Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII is received by the Designated Individual, individually or on behalf of the Contractor, the Designated Individual shall immediately notify the TJPA thereof in order to permit the TJPA to investigate the circumstances, prepare any appropriate documentation, seek to quash the subpoena, to seek a protective order, or to take such other action regarding the request as it deems appropriate; and the undersigned will fully cooperate in the TJPA’s efforts in this regard.
perturbation.changed_text: If a subpoena, discovery request, Court order, Freedom Of Information Act Request, or any other request or demand authorized by law seeking disclosure of the Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII is received by the Designated Individual, individually or on behalf of the Contractor, the Designated Individual shall not notify the TJPA thereof and will not permit the TJPA to investigate the circumstances, prepare any appropriate documentation, seek to quash the subpoena, to seek a protective order, or to take such other action regarding the request as it deems appropriate; and the undersigned will not cooperate in the TJPA’s efforts in this regard.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the 'shall immediately notify' to 'shall not notify', creates a direct contradiction. This pertubation makes a contradiction with Section 7 which discusses Violations And Remedies, making the previous section completely redundant. The original text made sure to disclose any wrong doings. The current text makes sure to hide all possible disclosure attempts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Violations And Remedies. Unauthorized Disclosure: If a Contractor or any Designated Individual knows or has a reasonable belief that documents subject to the Specification have been or may be disclosed to an unauthorized person(s), the Contractor and/or Designated Individual shall imme diately notify the TJPA. The TJPA shall make the final determination as to whether a violation of the Specification occurred and the course of action to address the violation. Remedies: Any Contractor or Designated Individual that violates the Specificat ion with respect to Confidential Information, SSI and/or PCII shall, at the TJPA’s election, be subject to any of the following: (1) written reprimand of the Contractor and/or Designated Individual; (2) termination of the Contractor or Designated Individual’s access to Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII; (3) directive to the Contractor to immediately suspended the Designated Individual from performing any further work relating to the Project; (4) if a contract has been awarded, treatment of the unautho rized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, or PCII as a material breach of the contract, warranting the termination of the contract for cause; (5) commencement of legal proceedings for any other legal rights and remedies, including injunctive relie f and monetary damages arising from the breach of contract; (6) reporting of any violation related to SSI or PCII to the Department of Homeland Security, the Transportation Security Administration, or the Department of Transportation (these agencies may also independently assess penalties and/or take corrective action as provided under Federal law, including 49 C.F.R. §§ 15.17 and 1520.17); (7) disqualification of a Contractor from bidding on any work for the Project; and/or (8) written directive to retriev e all Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII disclosed to unauthorized persons at Contractor’s expense and to cease future unauthorized disclosure. If legal proceedings to enforce any remedy set forth under this paragraph are commenced by the TJPA, the prevailing party in such legal action shall be entitled to reasonable attorney’s fees and costs . Furthermore, Contractor agrees to indemnify the TJPA for any expenses or damages (including attorney’s fees or costs) incurred by the TJPA arising out of or relating to the Contractor and/or Designated Individual’s unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information, SSI, and/or PCII.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text requires the Designated Individual to notify the TJPA of any requests for disclosure of confidential information. The changed text explicitly states that the Designated Individual shall *not* notify the TJPA in such cases. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which requires immediate notification to the TJPA if there's a belief that documents subject to the Specification have been or may be disclosed to an unauthorized person, thereby making the 'Violations and Remedies' section redundant and unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of a Structural Flaw, as the modified clause creates conflicting implications with existing clauses, causing an in-text contradiction.
